title: Chaetogastra durangensis
intro: Chaetogastra durangensis is a plant species within the Asteraceae family, commonly referred to as a wild plant or weed. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and serving as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Chaetogastra durangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to leverage its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Chaetogastra durangensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to Mexico. The classification of Chaetogastra durangensis is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Gentianales, family - Apocynaceae, genus - Chaetogastra, species - C. durangensis.
